meaning: To reduce in amount or intensity
example: The storm finally began to abate by dawn.

meaning: A deviation from what is normal or expected
example: His rude reply was an aberration from his usual politeness.
trick: 'ab-' = away; erring away from the norm.

example: A true officer abhors cowardice and dishonesty.

meaning: To choose not to do something
example: He decided to abstain from alcohol during training.
The two nations signed an accord to end the conflict.
A leader needs business acumen to make quick decisions.
trick: 'a-CUE-men' = a man who takes the right cue = sharp.

example: He was adamant about following the rules.
She is adept at solving problems under pressure.
The coach admonished the team for being late.
True character is revealed in times of adversity.

meaning: Friendly and easy to talk to
example: The new captain was affable and approachable.
Skipping rest will only aggravate the injury.
Rest can alleviate the pain after a long march.
He stayed aloof from the group during the camp.

meaning: Having more than one meaning; unclear
example: His ambiguous answer confused the panel.
The climb was arduous but rewarding.
trick: 'ar-DUE-ous' = a task that takes its dues from you.

example: A good leader can articulate a clear vision.
An astute commander spots danger early.
The audacious plan surprised the enemy.
trick: 'audi-' to dare; bold enough to be heard.

example: They augmented the force with fresh troops.

The board concurred with the decision.

meaning: To accept or forgive wrong behaviour

example: His bravery was conspicuous in battle.

meaning: A feeling that someone is worthless
example: He looked at the traitor with contempt.
trick: 'con-TEMPT' = you 'tempt' to look down on it.
'convince' = a belief you are convinced of.

example: She took copious notes during training.

meaning: The ability to face danger or fear
trick: 'cour-' = heart (French coeur); acting from the heart.

example: The witness gave a credible account.
They curtailed the leave due to the alert.

meaning: To postpone; or to yield respectfully
example: The meeting was deferred to Monday.
trick: 'de-FAR' = push it farther away in time.

example: Her deft handling saved the mission.
The defiant recruit refused the order.

meaning: Done on purpose; or to think carefully
example: It was a deliberate act of sabotage.
The team demolished the old bunker.

meaning: To use up or reduce greatly
example: The long march depleted their water supply.
We deplore all acts of terror.

meaning: To look down on with contempt

example: Lack of sleep is detrimental to focus.

example: His silence was an enigma to all.
